verses.commentary: It was just before the Passover Festival. Jesus knew that the hour had come for Him to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the end. The hour arrives. Departure imminent. Eis telos (εἰς τέλος), to the end or to the uttermost. Love's full extent demonstrated.

The evening meal was in progress, and the devil had already prompted Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, to betray Jesus. Last Supper begins. Satan's work in Judas. Betrayal planned. Evil infiltrates the meal.

Jesus knew that the Father had put all things under His power, and that He had come from God and was returning to God. Full divine consciousness. Authority complete. Origin clear. Destination certain. From God, to God. Incarnation bracketed by eternity.
So He got up from the meal, took off His outer clothing, and wrapped a towel around His waist. Deliberate action. Removes garments. Dresses as slave. Role reversal begins.

After that, He poured water into a basin and began to wash His disciples' feet, drying them with the towel that was wrapped around Him. Slave's task. Master serves. Shocking reversal. Humility enacted. Cleansing performed.
He came to Simon Peter, who said to Him, Lord, are You going to wash my feet? Peter's protest. Discomfort at role reversal. Lord should not serve. Hierarchy challenged.
Jesus replied, You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand. Present confusion. Future clarity. Resurrection brings comprehension. Symbolic meaning hidden now.

No, You shall never wash my feet. Jesus answered, Unless I wash you, you have no part with Me. Peter's adamant refusal. Jesus' absolute requirement. Washing is non-negotiable. Spiritual cleansing essential. No part (meros, μέρος), no share in Jesus without His cleansing.
Then, Lord, not just my feet but my hands and my head as well! Peter's reversal. From rejection to excess. Wants total washing. All or nothing personality.

Jesus answered, Those who have had a bath need only to wash their feet; their whole body is clean. And you are clean, though not every one of you. Bath versus foot-washing. Initial cleansing versus ongoing cleansing. Regeneration versus daily sanctification. Not all clean: Judas excluded.
For He knew who was going to betray Him, and that was why He said not every one was clean. Divine foreknowledge. Judas identified. Betrayer among the clean. Evil within community.

When He had finished washing their feet, He put on His clothes and returned to His place. Do you understand what I have done for you? Resume normal position. Question posed. Comprehension tested. Meaning must be grasped.

You call Me Teacher and Lord, and rightly so, for that is what I am. Titles affirmed. Teacher (didaskalos, διδάσκαλος) and Lord (kyrios, κύριος). Authority acknowledged. Jesus validates their names for Him.
Now that I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another's feet. Command issued. If Master serves, servants must serve. Mutual service required. Hierarchy flattened.
I have set you an example that you should do as I have done for you. Hypodeigma (ὑπόδειγμα), example, pattern. Imitation demanded. Model provided. Do likewise.

Very truly I tell you, no servant is greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him. Hierarchical principle. Servant cannot claim exemption from what Master does. If Jesus serves, so must they.
Now that you know these things, you will be blessed if you do them. Knowledge plus action equals blessing. Doing, not just knowing. Obedience brings blessing. James 1:22-25.

I am not referring to all of you; I know those I have chosen. But this is to fulfill this passage of Scripture: He who shared My bread has turned against Me. Exclusion. Chosen ones identified. Scripture fulfills. Psalm 41:9. Betrayal prophesied. Shared bread: covenant intimacy violated.

I am telling you now before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I am who I am. Foreknowledge proves identity. Prediction validates. Egō eimi (ἐγώ εἰμι). Divine name. Fulfilled prophecy builds faith.

Very truly I tell you, whoever accepts anyone I send accepts Me; and whoever accepts Me accepts the One who sent Me. Chain of representation. Apostolic authority. Receiving sent ones receives Sender. Rejection of messengers rejects God.

After He had said this, Jesus was troubled in spirit and testified, Very truly I tell you, one of you is going to betray Me. Tarasso (ταράσσω), deeply disturbed. Betrayal grieves Jesus. One of you: the shock. Inner circle includes traitor.
His disciples stared at one another, at a loss to know which of them He meant. Confusion. Mutual suspicion. No one certain. Self-examination begins.
One of them, the disciple whom Jesus loved, was reclining next to Him. Beloved disciple. John's self-designation. Reclining position: intimacy. Proximity to Jesus.
Simon Peter motioned to this disciple and said, Ask Him which one He means. Peter's initiative. Curiosity. Fear. Need to know. Beloved disciple as intermediary.
Leaning back against Jesus, he asked Him, Lord, who is it? Physical closeness. Direct question. Intimacy allows inquiry.

Jesus answered, It is the one to whom I will give this piece of bread when I have dipped it in the dish. Then, dipping the piece of bread, He gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. Sign given. Bread dipped. Normally honor. Here, identification. Judas receives morsel. Warning and love combined.

As soon as Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. So Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly. Demonic possession. Satan enters. Jesus commands. Acceleration of evil. Permission given to execute plan. Divine sovereignty over betrayal timing.
But no one at the meal understood why Jesus said this to him. Disciples remain ignorant. Hidden meaning. They miss the signal.

Since Judas had charge of the money, some thought Jesus was telling him to buy what was needed for the festival, or to give something to the poor. Innocent interpretation. They assume normal transaction. Financial duty. Charitable act. They cannot imagine betrayal.

As soon as Judas had taken the bread, he went out. And it was night. Immediate departure. Night: literal and symbolic. Darkness. Evil's hour. John's theological commentary. Judas enters spiritual night.
When he was gone, Jesus said, Now the Son of Man is glorified and God is glorified in Him. Betrayer exits. Glorification begins. Cross approaches. God glorified through suffering. Paradoxical glory.
If God is glorified in Him, God will glorify the Son in Himself, and will glorify Him at once. Mutual glorification. Father glorifies Son. Immediately. Resurrection and exaltation imminent.

My children, I will be with you only a little longer. You will look for Me, and just as I told the Jews, so I tell you now: Where I am going, you cannot come. Tender address. Teknion (τεκνίον), little children. Brief time remains. Departure announced. Heaven inaccessible for now.

A new command I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you must love one another. New commandment. Kainē (καινή), new in quality, not time. Leviticus 19:18 elevated. Standard: Jesus' love. Model provided. Sacrificial, serving love.
By this everyone will know that you are My disciples, if you love one another. Distinguishing mark. Love authenticates. Visible proof of discipleship. Community love is evangelistic witness.

Simon Peter asked Him, Lord, where are You going? Jesus replied, Where I am going, you cannot follow now, but you will follow later. Peter's question. Destination inquiry. Not now: timing. Later: promise. Peter will follow to death. John 21:18-19.
Peter asked, Lord, why can't I follow You now? I will lay down my life for You. Bold claim. Confident assertion. Willing to die. Sincere but self-deceived. He doesn't know his weakness.

Then Jesus answered, Will you really lay down your life for Me? Very truly I tell you, before the rooster crows, you will disown Me three times! Challenge to boast. Prediction of denial. Before rooster crows: same night. Three times: specific number. Peter's failure foretold. Pride precedes fall.
